name: CASTLEMAN DISEASE
definition: ANGIOFOLLICULAR LYMPH NODE HYPERPLASIA = BENIGN GIANT LYMPH NODE HYPERPLASIA = diverse group of rare nonclonal lymphoproliferative disorders of differing histopathologic plasmablastic variant (= microlymphoma) in immunosuppressed patients with positive potentially malignant lymphoproliferative disorder
dominant_finding: spotty coarse peripheral / central branching calcifications (10-31%)
discriminating_features: enlarged lymph node with preserved architecture

general: enlarged lymph node with preserved architecture
US: increased vascularity on color Doppler with large vessels at periphery extending
CT: sharply marginated smooth / lobulated homogeneous solitary mass of muscle density
spotty coarse peripheral / central branching calcifications (10-31%)
intense enhancement almost equal to aorta (in hyaline vascular type)
nonenhancing central areas ← fibrosis / cystic necrosis
MR: heterogeneous mass iso- / hyperintense to muscle on T1WI
T2-hypointense stellate scar = central area / linear web of hypointensity ← fibrosis /
scar hypointense on early phase dynamic T1WI
peripheral flow voids of feeding vessels surrounding mass (in hyaline vascular type)
ANGIO: hypervascular mass with intense homogeneous blush (hyalin-vascular type)
enlarged feeding vessels arising from bronchial / internal mammary / intercostal arteries
1-6 cm large homogeneous lymph nodes in multiple mediastinal compartments
cysts ← partial airway obstruction by peribronchial + peribronchiolar LIP
